name: Navigate to sign up from landing page
description: Find and click sign-up link from the landing page to create an account
persona: Prospective user who has read about OpenBat and wants to create a free account
preconditions: User is on the landing page

steps.action: Visit the landing page and verify navigation links are visible
Click 'Sign Up' in the top navigation bar
Verify the sign-up page loads with the account creation form

expectedOutcome: User lands on the sign-up page ready to create an account

failureModes.trigger: 'Start Free' and 'Get Started' buttons are placeholder links (#link)
failureModes.result: Clicking 'Start Free' or most 'Get Started' buttons does not navigate — only nav 'Sign Up' and Free tier 'Get Started' work

alternativePaths: Click 'Get Started' on Free pricing tier (only one that works)

name: Explore feature demos
description: Toggle between demo scenarios on the landing page to understand product value
persona: Prospective customer evaluating the product before signing up
preconditions: User is on the landing page

steps.action: Scroll to the 'From raw conversation to actionable insight' section
Click 'Missed Upsell', 'Feature Gap Risk', and 'Bot Hallucination' buttons
Verify demo content changes between scenarios showing conversation, prompt fix, and Slack alert

expectedOutcome: User sees different AI analysis scenarios and understands the product value proposition
relatedFlows: Navigate to sign up from landing page

name: Email login
description: Authenticate using email and password
persona: Returning user who already has an account
preconditions: User is not already logged in

steps.action: Navigate to /auth/login and verify the login form is visible
Enter email address in the Email field
Enter password in the Password field
Click 'Login' button
Verify redirect to /platform dashboard with chatbots visible

expectedOutcome: User is redirected to /platform and sees their dashboard with chatbots

failureModes.result: Alert shown: 'Invalid login credentials' — stays on /auth/login

name: Social auth login
description: Authenticate using a social provider (Apple, Google, or Meta)
persona: User who prefers social login
preconditions: User has linked their social account

steps.action: Verify social auth buttons exist (Apple, Google, Meta)
Click Google social auth button

expectedOutcome: User is authenticated and redirected to /platform

failureModes.trigger: Click social auth button in localhost without OAuth provider configured
failureModes.result: Button click does not navigate — likely requires Supabase OAuth provider configuration

name: Forgot password flow
description: Navigate from login to password reset page
persona: User who has forgotten their password

steps.action: Navigate to /auth/login
Click 'Forgot your password?' link
Verify landing on password reset page with email field

expectedOutcome: User lands on the password reset page to enter their email

name: Email sign up
description: Create a new account with email and password
persona: New user who wants to create an account
preconditions: User does not have an existing account

steps.action: Navigate to /auth/sign-up and verify form fields
Enter email, password, and confirm password
Click 'Create Account'

expectedOutcome: Account is created and user is redirected to the platform

failureModes.result: Alert: 'Password should be at least 6 characters.' Note: UI says 8 but backend enforces 6

relatedFlows: Navigate to sign up from landing page

name: Password reset
description: Request a password reset email
persona: User who has forgotten their password
preconditions: User has an existing account with a valid email

steps.action: Navigate to /auth/forgot-password
Enter email and click 'Send reset email'
Verify confirmation message: 'Check Your Email'

expectedOutcome: Confirmation page: 'Password reset instructions sent'

name: Open a chatbot
description: Navigate from dashboard to a chatbot's analytics page
persona: Analyst reviewing chatbot performance data

steps.action: Navigate to /platform and see chatbot cards
Click '...' menu on chatbot card
Click 'Open chatbot' from context menu
Verify chatbot dashboard loads with KPIs, sentiment charts, and analysis data

expectedOutcome: User sees chatbot dashboard with KPIs (373 conversations, 1292 messages), sentiment chart, frustrations, dissatisfied users, topics, revenue signals, intents, heatmap, languages

name: Create a new chatbot
description: Create a new chatbot from the dashboard
persona: Developer or admin setting up OpenBat for a new project

steps.action: Navigate to /platform
Click '+ New chatbot' button, modal opens with name field
Enter chatbot name and click 'Continue to setup'

expectedOutcome: Chatbot created, redirected to 6-step onboarding wizard (Basics, Product, Brand, Pricing, Context, Connect)

name: Switch organization
description: Switch between organizations using the sidebar switcher
persona: User who manages multiple teams or products

steps.action: Click organization switcher in sidebar
View dropdown with organizations list and 'Create organization' option

expectedOutcome: Dashboard updates to show chatbots for the selected organization

failureModes.result: Dropdown shows only current org and 'Create organization' option — no switching possible

alternativePaths: Create a new organization from the switcher

name: Invite a team member
description: Invite a new member to the organization
persona: Organization admin who wants to give team members access
preconditions: User has Owner or admin role

steps.action: Navigate to /platform/members and see members table
Click 'Invite member' button — modal opens with email and role fields
Verify role dropdown has Admin and Member options

expectedOutcome: Invitation sent to email, member appears in table once accepted

name: Rename organization
description: Update the organization name in settings
persona: Organization owner updating the org name

steps.action: Navigate to /platform/settings and see org name field with 'Test' value
Edit name field and click Save

expectedOutcome: Organization name is updated across the platform

name: Delete organization
description: Permanently delete the organization and all data
persona: Organization owner who wants to remove the organization

steps.action: Navigate to /platform/settings
Click 'Delete organization' in danger zone
Confirmation dialog requires typing org name to confirm — Delete button disabled until matched

expectedOutcome: Organization and all data permanently deleted

name: Investigate a dissatisfied user
description: Click through from dissatisfied user list to their conversations
persona: Customer success manager investigating negative experiences

steps.action: Navigate to chatbot dashboard, see 'Most dissatisfied users' section
Click 'Investigate →' next to Riccardo G (score 4.1)
Conversations page loads but shows ALL conversations (116 rows), not filtered to user

expectedOutcome: Conversations page opens filtered to show only that user's conversations

failureModes.trigger: URL has ?user= query param but table shows all conversations unfiltered
failureModes.result: BUG: The user filter query parameter is not being applied — all 116 conversations shown instead of just Riccardo's

name: Change analysis time period
description: Change the dashboard date range to filter analytics data
persona: Analyst comparing metrics across different time periods
preconditions: User is on the chatbot dashboard

steps.action: Click 'This month' date range button to open date picker
Date picker opens with presets: Today, Yesterday, This week, Last week, This month, Last month, This year, Last year, All time, Custom. Dual calendar grid.
Select 'Last week' and click Apply — dashboard data updates (frustrations: 3 vs 5, users: 1 vs 3, languages: en 100%)

expectedOutcome: All dashboard KPIs, charts, and tables update to reflect the selected date range

name: Create a workflow from template
description: Use a pre-built template to create an automated workflow
persona: Customer success lead who wants automated notifications

steps.action: Navigate to workflows page and see Workflows/Runs/Templates tabs
Click Templates tab — see 6 templates with category filters (Alerting, Escalation, Reporting, Moderation, General)

expectedOutcome: Templates available: Negative Sentiment Alert, Escalation on Frustration, Topic Spike Monitor, Chatbot Quality Gate, Hallucination Flag, Positive Feedback Tracker

name: Create a workflow from scratch
description: Create a custom workflow with name, conditions, and actions
persona: Power user who needs custom automation

steps.action: Click 'Create workflow' button on Workflows tab — inline form appears with name field and Create/Cancel buttons

expectedOutcome: Workflow created and available for configuration

name: View conversation detail
description: Open a conversation to see the full transcript with analysis annotations
persona: Analyst reviewing specific customer interactions
preconditions: Chatbot has at least one conversation

steps.action: Navigate to conversations page (116 rows, paginated)
Click on Riccardo G's conversation row

Conversation detail loads with full transcript, per-message tags (Very Negative, Complaint, System Support and Feedback, Dodging, Deflected, brand voice violation), sidebar with sentiment score (-0.90), user info, org info, session data

expectedOutcome: Full conversation with analysis annotations and metadata sidebar

name: Filter conversations
description: Use search and filters to narrow conversation results
persona: Analyst looking for specific types of conversations

steps.action: Conversations page has search box, Filter button, sortable columns (User, Email, Organization, Messages, Sentiment, Language, Plan, Last Message, Created)

expectedOutcome: Table updates to show only conversations matching filter criteria

name: Review message-level analysis
description: View detailed AI analysis tags on individual messages
persona: QA analyst investigating AI analysis decisions
preconditions: User is on a conversation detail page

steps.action: On conversation detail, clickable analysis tags visible on messages: 'Very Negative', 'Complaint', 'System Support and Feedback' (user), 'Dodging', 'Deflected', 'brand voice violation' (assistant)

expectedOutcome: Analysis tags are clickable and show reasoning for each annotation

name: Semantic search conversations
description: Search conversations by meaning using semantic similarity
persona: Product manager looking for thematic patterns
preconditions: Chatbot has conversations with embedded semantic data

steps.action: Navigate to Deep Search page with semantic search field, date range picker, and 'Deep Search' button

expectedOutcome: Conversations ranked by semantic relevance to the query

name: Browse client organizations
description: View and filter customer organizations with sortable metrics
persona: Customer success manager monitoring account health
preconditions: Chatbot has conversations with organization metadata

steps.action: Navigate to organizations page — 48 rows with columns: Organization, Plan, Industry, MRR, Conversations, Users, Sentiment, Last Activity

expectedOutcome: Sortable, filterable table of customer organizations

name: Browse chatbot users
description: View and filter individual chatbot users with sentiment data
persona: Customer success manager tracking individual user satisfaction
preconditions: Chatbot has conversations with user metadata

steps.action: Navigate to users page — 26 rows with columns: User, Email, Organization, Plan, MRR, Conversations, Sentiment, Last Activity

expectedOutcome: Sortable, filterable table of chatbot users

name: Configure user analysis flags
description: Toggle and customize analysis flags for user messages
persona: Product manager customizing signal detection
preconditions: User has access to analysis config

steps.action: Navigate to analysis config — tabs: Metadata, Translation, User Analysis, Assistant Analysis, Prompts

expectedOutcome: User Analysis tab allows toggling flags and creating custom ones

name: Enable auto-translation
description: Enable automatic translation of non-primary-language messages
persona: Product team with international users
preconditions: Chatbot receives messages in multiple languages

steps.action: Click Translation tab in analysis config

expectedOutcome: Translation settings with toggle and language selection

name: Edit analysis prompts
description: Customize the AI prompts used for conversation analysis
persona: Power user or ML engineer fine-tuning analysis

steps.action: Click Prompts tab in analysis config

expectedOutcome: Prompt editor with Sentiment, Intent, Topics, Flags cards

name: Track metadata fields
description: Accept discovered metadata fields to make them filterable
persona: Developer who has set up SDK with custom metadata

steps.action: Metadata tab shows Managed fields (custom.templateId, custom.templateName) and 8 Discovered fields (browser, custom.chatbot, device, ip, language, os, referer, userAgent) with Track buttons

expectedOutcome: Tracked fields become available as filter columns in conversations table

name: Configure API keys
description: View and rotate API keys for SDK authentication
persona: Developer setting up or maintaining SDK integration
preconditions: User has access to chatbot settings

steps.action: Navigate to settings, click API Keys tab — shows masked key prefix ob_live_8ae071a6 with copy and Rotate key buttons

expectedOutcome: API key visible (masked) with copy and rotate functionality

name: Configure webhooks
description: Add webhook destinations for workflow alerts
persona: Operations lead setting up Slack/Discord alerts

steps.action: Click Webhooks tab — empty state with 'Add webhook' button and docs for Discord, Slack, and Custom types

expectedOutcome: Webhook destination saved and available for workflow actions

name: Export conversation data
description: Download conversations and analysis results as JSON
persona: Data analyst running custom analysis or admin creating backup

steps.action: Click Import/Export Data tab — Export section with JSON format selector, 'Include analysis results' checkbox, '1,292 messages' count, Export button

expectedOutcome: JSON file with all conversations and analysis results downloads

name: Import conversation data
description: Upload historical conversations for analysis
persona: Developer with historical conversation logs
preconditions: User has conversation data in JSON or CSV

steps.action: Import section on Import/Export Data tab — 'Download template CSV' button, file chooser, Import button (disabled until file selected)

expectedOutcome: Conversations imported and available for analysis
